messages.content: rewrite this:The day featured complimentary examinations and exclusive discounts at the NWN center for all students, families, and staff members of the high school. Additionally, the event included informative lectures followed by comprehensive eye-checks for students, families, and staff, further encouraging preventative healthcare and inspiring healthier lifestyles.

The NWN center hosted a special day for high school students, families, and staff, offering free examinations and discounts. The event also included educational talks and thorough eye exams, promoting preventive healthcare and healthier living.

rewrite this:The International School of Business (ISB) at the Modern University for Business and Science (MUBS), in collaboration with the National Wellness Network (NWN), organized a training event on April 12, 2024, at the Lebanese International College (LIC) as part of the National Education Platform Initiative (NEP). The training, titled "Enhancing Management, Communication, and Customer Service Skills for Schools," aimed to provide LIC administrative staff with valuable knowledge and skills necessary for effective school leadership.

The ISB at MUBS, in conjunction with NWN, arranged a training session on April 12, 2024, at LIC for the NEP. The event, called "Improving Management, Communication, and Customer Service Skills for Schools," was designed to equip LIC administrative staff with essential expertise for successful school management.

rewrite this:
Mrs. Razan Baradhi, Acting Chairperson of the Management Department at ISB, delivered the training titled "Enhancing Management, Communication, and Customer Service Skills for Schools", focused on the challenges in school management and explored principles of educational management. The training also emphasized essential managerial skills, effective communication strategies, and time management techniques, with interactive activities and games incorporated to enhance participants’ understanding.

During the training session titled "Improving Management, Communication, and Customer Service Skills for Schools," Mrs. Razan Baradhi, the Acting Chairperson of the Management Department at ISB, discussed the difficulties in school management and examined educational management principles. The training highlighted crucial managerial abilities, efficient communication tactics, and time management methods, incorporating interactive exercises and games to deepen participants' comprehension.

rewrite this quotation: “To be selected by AARU to lead an effort to develop a culture of sustainability across the Arab world is an honor.” said Prof. Alami. “The university considers sustainability as a pillar in its strategic plan. We have been working tirelessly for several years now to promote sustainability across Lebanon, especially focusing on quality education, gender equality, access to quality education, countering poverty, and establishing partnerships with local and international organizations that share our mission. This is an opportunity for the university community to also promote a similar culture across the Arab world, and I look forward to working with my honorable colleagues in this council to respond to the needs of the community and promote sustainability.”

"It is a privilege to be chosen by AARU to spearhead the initiative aimed at fostering a culture of sustainability throughout the Arab world," declared Prof. Alami. "Sustainability holds a crucial place in the university's strategic agenda. Our dedicated efforts over the years have been focused on advancing sustainability in Lebanon, with particular emphasis on providing quality education, advocating for gender equality, enhancing access to education, combating poverty, and forging partnerships with local and international entities that align with our mission. This endeavor presents an opportunity for the university community to further propagate a similar ethos throughout the Arab region. I eagerly anticipate collaborating with esteemed colleagues on the council to meet the community's needs and champion sustainability."
rewrite again but do not stray too much from the original

Prof. Alami expressed, "Being selected by AARU to lead the development of a sustainability culture in the Arab world is truly an honor. Sustainability is a cornerstone of our university's strategic plan, and we have been diligently promoting it in Lebanon for years. Our focus areas include quality education, gender equality, access to education, poverty alleviation, and partnerships with like-minded organizations. This opportunity will allow us to extend our efforts to the wider Arab community, and I am excited to collaborate with my esteemed colleagues on the council to address community needs and advance sustainability."

rewrite this:During the annual meeting held on March 14, 2024, the Modern University for Business and Science (MUBS) Board of Trustees appointed Prof. Nael Alami as the new president of the university, effective July 2024. Upon the completion of his tenure, Prof. Ali Cheaib, the current MUBS President, will be joining the ranks of the MUBS Board of Trustees.

The meeting was attended by Prof. Hatem Alamy, Chairman of the Board of Trustees; Dr. Bassem Kaissi, Vice President of the Board of Trustees; Prof. Ali Cheaib, University President; Prof. Nael Alami, University Vice President; Mrs. Sahira Mtaweh, Vice President for Administrative Affairs; MP Dr. Fadi Alameh; Dr. Munir Hamza; Talat Lahham; Maher Asaad Saqqal; and Kamal Al-Ahmadiyya, the Secretary of the Board.

During the annual meeting on March 14, 2024, the Board of Trustees at the Modern University for Business and Science (MUBS) appointed Prof. Nael Alami as the incoming university president, effective from July 2024. After completing his term, Prof. Ali Cheaib, the current MUBS President, will transition to the MUBS Board of Trustees.

Present at the meeting were Prof. Hatem Alamy, the Board of Trustees Chairman; Dr. Bassem Kaissi, the Board of Trustees Vice President; Prof. Ali Cheaib, the University President; Prof. Nael Alami, the University Vice President; Mrs. Sahira Mtaweh, the Vice President for Administrative Affairs; MP Dr. Fadi Alameh; Dr. Munir Hamza; Talat Lahham; Maher Asaad Saqqal; and Kamal Al-Ahmadiyya, the Board's Secretary.

Present at the meeting were Prof. Hatem Alamy, Chairman, BOT; Dr. Bassem Kaissi, Vice President, BOT; Prof. Ali Cheaib, University President; Prof. Nael Alami, University Vice President; Mrs. Sahira Mtaweh, Vice President for Administrative Affairs; MP Dr. Fadi Alameh; Dr. Munir Hamza; Talat Lahham; Maher Asaad Saqqal; and Kamal Al-Ahmadiyya, the BOT’s Secretary.

During the meeting, Prof. Hatem Alamy discussed the university's vision, highlighting its progress and future plans. He also emphasized the expansion plan, which has been put into action through the National Education Platform (NEP). This platform includes partnership agreements with over a hundred secondary schools and institutes, aiming to strengthen the university's position and partnerships in the job market, as well as create employment opportunities for graduates and enhance the university's role in development.
 
Prof. Nael Alami, in turn, outlined the future strategies to enhance the university's operations, focusing on filling academic and administrative positions. He also highlighted the importance of scientific research, a research-oriented educational technology plan, and strengthening international partnerships with universities worldwide. Additionally, he emphasized the significance of investing in international relations, particularly with European universities and Arab university associations, as well as sustainable development activities in various regions.

Present at the meeting were Prof. Hatem Alamy, Chairman, Board of Trustees (BOT); Dr. Bassem Kaissi, Vice President, BOT; Prof. Ali Cheaib, University President; Prof. Nael Alami, University Vice President; Mrs. Sahira Mtaweh, Vice President for Administrative Affairs; MP Dr. Fadi Alameh; Dr. Munir Hamza; Talat Lahham; Maher Asaad Saqqal; and Kamal Al-Ahmadiyya, Secretary of the BOT.

Prof. Hatem Alamy led discussions during the meeting on the university's vision, highlighting its advancements and future initiatives. He particularly stressed the ongoing implementation of the expansion plan, as part of the National Education Platform (NEP). This platform has forged partnership agreements with over a hundred secondary schools and institutes, aimed at bolstering the university's standing, fostering job market partnerships, generating employment opportunities for graduates, and enhancing the university's developmental role.

Subsequently, Prof. Nael Alami outlined forthcoming strategies to elevate the university's operations, with a focus on faculty and administrative recruitments. He underscored the importance of scientific research, a research-focused educational technology strategy, and the reinforcement of global partnerships with universities worldwide. Further, he emphasized the importance of investing in international relations, particularly with European universities and Arab university associations, alongside promoting sustainable development initiatives across various regions.

Prof. Alami brings a wealth of expertise and a strong research background to his new role as president. He is a molecular neuroscientist whose research interests fall at the intersection of the fields of neuroscience, global health, and the learning sciences. He is the founder of the Virtual Exchange Program (VEP) and the Center of Excellence in Research, Education, and Cultural Studies (CEREC). He holds a PhD in Molecular Neuroscience from the Ohio State University, a Master’s degree in Education Policy, Organization, and Leadership Studies from Stanford University, and served as a post-doctoral fellow at St. Jude Children's Research Hospital in Memphis, Tennessee. He was also a visiting scientist at Harvard University and Columbia University, a visiting lecturer at San Jose State University in California, and a guest lecturer at Stanford University.
 
Prof. Nael Alami's appointment as the new MUBS President sets the stage for a promising future as the university continues to strive for academic excellence, research advancement, and valuable partnerships locally and internationally.

With a wealth of expertise and a robust research background, Prof. Alami assumes his new role as president. He is a molecular neuroscientist with research interests spanning neuroscience, global health, and the learning sciences. Prof. Alami is credited as the founder of the Virtual Exchange Program (VEP) and the Center of Excellence in Research, Education, and Cultural Studies (CEREC). He obtained his PhD in Molecular Neuroscience from the Ohio State University, a Master’s degree in Education Policy, Organization, and Leadership Studies from Stanford University, and served as a post-doctoral fellow at St. Jude Children's Research Hospital in Memphis, Tennessee. He has held visiting scientist positions at Harvard University and Columbia University, served as a visiting lecturer at San Jose State University in California, and acted as a guest lecturer at Stanford University.

The appointment of Prof. Nael Alami as the new MUBS President heralds a promising chapter as the university continues its pursuit of academic excellence, research progress, and valuable partnerships at local and global levels.

summarize and rewrite this:Dr. Alami started his career as a developmental neurobiologist at St Jude Children’s Research Hospital in Memphis, Tennessee, USA, and is a trained molecular neuroscientist who received his doctoral degree from the Molecular, Cellular, and Developmental Biology Program where he studied slow axonal transport within the Department of Neuroscience at The Ohio State University. While at St Jude, Dr. Alami was also a visiting scientist at Harvard University and Columbia University, where he developed novel technologies to identify human motor neurons and live neuronal imaging approaches that contribute to the search of a cure to neurodegenerative diseases like ALS and FTLD. After his work at St Jude, Dr. Alami completed a third graduate degree in Policy, Organization, and Leadership Studies from Stanford University in Palo Alto, where he became interested in the use of education technology and innovative new practices to increase educational access, and its use in promoting global health and cultural understanding. He launched the Student Virtual Exchange Program while at Stanford in partnership with Stanford educators, allowing students in Lebanon and American Universities to explore and challenge cultural perceptions and stereotypes that skewed their judgment about each other, in a safe, structured, academic environment. He was also a Visiting Professor at San Jose State University in California, teaching at the Connie L Lurie College of Education

Dr. Alami began his career as a developmental neurobiologist at St Jude Children’s Research Hospital in Memphis, Tennessee, USA. He holds a doctoral degree in Molecular, Cellular, and Developmental Biology from The Ohio State University, specializing in slow axonal transport within the Department of Neuroscience. During his time at St Jude, Dr. Alami was a visiting scientist at Harvard University and Columbia University, where he focused on developing advanced technologies for identifying human motor neurons and live neuronal imaging to aid in the quest for treatments for neurodegenerative diseases like ALS and FTLD.

Following his tenure at St Jude, Dr. Alami pursued a third graduate degree in Policy, Organization, and Leadership Studies at Stanford University in Palo Alto. It was during this time that his interest in education technology, innovative practices for enhancing educational access, and promoting global health and cultural understanding emerged. At Stanford, he launched the Student Virtual Exchange Program in collaboration with educators, offering students in Lebanon and American universities an opportunity to challenge cultural biases in a secure academic setting.

Additionally, Dr. Alami served as a Visiting Professor at San Jose State University in California, teaching at the Connie L Lurie College of Education.

summarize:Studying management is not just about acquiring knowledge; it's about developing the skills and mindset needed to navigate today's complex and rapidly changing business environment. In a world where innovation and adaptability are key drivers of success, the ability to think strategically, analyze data, and make informed decisions is more important than ever. Our program provides you with the tools and frameworks to tackle real-world challenges and drive positive change within organizations.

Our management program focuses on developing essential skills and a strategic mindset to navigate the fast-paced business landscape, emphasizing the importance of innovation, adaptability, strategic thinking, data analysis, and informed decision-making to drive organizational success and create positive change.

rewrite this:At MUBS, we are committed to providing students with an exceptional educational experience that will prepare them for success in the rapidly evolving field of computer science. Our programs are designed to equip you with both theoretical knowledge and practical skills, enabling you to thrive in a technology-driven world.

By choosing to pursue your education at MUBS, you will have access to a supportive learning environment, renowned faculty members, and a vibrant community of fellow students who share your passion for computer science. We are dedicated to empowering you to reach your full potential and to providing you with the tools and knowledge necessary to excel in your chosen field.

The Computer Science Department at MUBS provides a comprehensive curriculum that covers various areas of computer science, including artificial intelligence (AI), cybersecurity, and programming languages including Python, Java, C Sharp, and web design. I then made my way to the washing machine and refrigerator section, where a staff member approached me upon noticing me.

Shopper: I'm looking for a top-loading washing machine, latest model.
Staff: We have a top-loading model with dual doors for drying as well.
Shopper: Yes.
Staff: This model is priced at 3,999 Riyals, made in Turkey and assembled in China. It comes with a warranty from the dealer for two years covering maintenance and compressor for five years. Extra comprehensive warranties are available for 3, 4, or 5 years with maintenance fees or device replacements.

He introduced me to the Jood membership.
Jood Membership:
There are two packages available;
The Blue Package: Offering a 2% discount on all exhibition products excluding Apple products, 10% discount on services, after-sales services, and additional product services. It includes a Scaviz card for 200 Riyals, providing access to Hakir Games, and a one-month return and exchange policy.
The Gold Package:
With a 5% discount on all store products except Apple items, a 15% service discount, and a Scaviz card for 500 Riyals, including discounts on Hakir restaurants and hotels.
in a 1st person narrative paragraph in english

As I headed over to the washing machine and refrigerator section, a staff member approached me to offer assistance. I inquired about a modern top-loading washing machine, and he presented one with dual doors for drying, priced at 3,999 Riyals, manufactured in Turkey with Chinese assembly. The machine was backed by a dealer warranty covering maintenance for two years and compressor for five years, with additional extended warranties available for up to five years. He then introduced me to the Jood membership program, detailing the benefits of the Blue and Gold packages, each offering discounts on products, services, and exclusive perks for members.

I spoke with the cashier, Fahad, during my product return.

Shopper: I would like to exchange or return this.
Cashier: You have 7 days, but if it's opened and used, no returns. If opened but unused, there will be a 30% deduction. You need to have the receipt and product packaging.

The cashier smiled throughout the exchange.

I completed the refund at 7:58 PM.
in a 1st person narrative paragraph in english

I had a conversation with the cashier, Fahad, while processing my return. I inquired about exchanging or returning the item. Fahad explained that I had a 7-day window for returns, with a 30% deduction if the item was opened and unused. He kindly reminded me to have the receipt and original packaging. Fahad maintained a pleasant smile during our interaction. I completed the refund transaction at 7:58 PM.

Conversation:

Me: Hello, I need your assistance.
Him: Hello, how can I help you?
Me: I have a few inquiries, please.
Him: Sure.
Me: Is this the work station area?
Him: Yes.
Me: Could you please help me with office chairs you have?
Him: Here are the available chairs on your right and behind you.
Me: Great. Do these chairs come with their tables, or can I buy them separately?
Him: You can buy them as you prefer.
Me: What are the features of this chair compared to the others?
Him: Both options are good, but this one is more comfortable and looks better.
Me: Are these all the office chairs you have?
Him: No, there are other chairs in the self-service area over there (pointing).
Me: Thank you. Are these chairs of Swedish manufacture?
Him: Absolutely.
Me: Do these chairs come with the "Buy Now, Pay Later in 4 Instalments" option?
Him: Yes, it's available with the Tabby and Tamara services.
Me: Thank you very much.

I visited Dida, who showcased a variety of household items and directed me toward kitchen appliances and frying pans. I reached out to Dida after getting lost due to his colleague guiding me to a different section from where I wanted to be. When I asked about home decor, Dida confirmed that it included decorations, kitchen tools, storage solutions, bedroom essentials, and more. I expressed interest in frying pans, and he displayed pans of different sizes, recommending one of medium size. Dida informed me that as it's a passage section, there isn't a specific list for the household department. I thanked him for his assistance before leaving, and Dida appreciated my visit.

I approached the returns counter which was unstaffed, waited for 5 minutes, and then inquired at the checkout counter. The staff there directed me back to the returns counter, and called for Fred, who assisted me upon arrival. Fred welcomed me and inquired about my needs. I explained that I wished to return an item, declining any exchanges or purchases with the refund. Fred then inquired if I was an Ikea Family member, to which I replied no, declining to join. He asked if I wanted to shop for any other items at Ikea, even suggesting a cafetiere, to which I declined. He proceeded to request my signature, name, and mobile number for the return process, providing the refund with a receipt. Fred mentioned he couldn't provide the original receipt, thanked me for the transaction, and concluded the interaction.

In the electrical appliances department, I was assisted by an employee named Rawan Al-Salouli, a Saudi national with a wheat complexion, standing approximately 160 cm tall. I inquired about vacuum cleaners, and to understand my needs, she asked if I preferred a specific type. I mentioned that I needed a Japanese brand, and she recommended Panasonic and Hitachi models. Rawan explained the available promotions for Panasonic, its capacity in liters, and inquired about spare parts and insurance. She mentioned having Panasonic insurance, an Extra insurance, and elaborated on the Goud Plus and Goud Blue service differences. Reducing the vacuum cleaner's price to around 795 with a 7% Goud Plus discount or around 195 for Goud Blue with a 2% discount. Spare parts were confirmed to be available with a five-year warranty on the motor.

At the air conditioning section, I was attended to by an employee named Abdulaziz, of Egyptian nationality, with a dark complexion, and a height of approximately 175 cm. I greeted him and inquired about the split air conditioners. Abdulaziz mentioned the diverse selection available and inquired about the room's dimensions to determine the required cooling capacity. After confirming that I needed a cool setting only, he suggested various renowned brands like LG, Samsung, Gree, and Bosch, advising on energy efficiency and warranty details. Specifically, he recommended LG inverter models for energy savings, mentioning their gold and silver options with a 10-year motor warranty. Further, he explained the benefits of the Goud Plus and Goud Blue services, providing price details and energy-saving capabilities. The installation service included a free basic setup with additional copper piping and a free 4-meter installation. Abdulaziz also clarified options for external installations like wall brackets or balconies, suggesting wall brackets for outdoor setups and offering free installation. Additionally, he elaborated on the free delivery within a 60-kilometer radius and outlined payment systems available, including Tabby, Tamara, and the Extra-special Goud card in blue or gold variants, detailing their respective benefits and costs. I thanked him for his assistance.

Use the following information to create an instagram caption that will be posted on the MUBS page:

Congratulations to our Graphic Design students!
Organized by the AUB Nature Conservation Center
MUBS ranked 1st In the Annual International Biodiversity Day (IBDAA) 2024 in the category of Art & Design.

 wanted to share some exciting news with you regarding our Graphic Design Department's participation in the IBDAA competition 2024 at AUB. Our department was represented by a talented group consisting of Kinda Sayegh, Daniel Khaddaj, Enas Sayegh, Rima Mohtar, Mahmoud Jaber, Ribal Sarielddine, Maya Mlaeb, Assil Sukkara, Dima Ghraizi, and Dalal Ghanam.

‏I'm thrilled to inform you that all of our participants submitted outstanding posters, showcasing their creativity and skill. Even more notably, Ribal Sarielddine emerged as the winner of the first prize! This is a testament to the hard work and dedication of our team, as well as the quality of education and training they receive here at our department.
